warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.sourceTitle: Standard for Food Additive Use - GB2760-2015
rules.notes: Specific limits and conditions for food additive use must be verified against the full text of GB 2760-2015.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standard for Food Additive Use - GB2760-2015

rules.limit: Verification required against applicable GB Food Safety Standards
rules.sourceTitle: National Health Commission of the People's Republic of China
rules.notes: The National Health Commission is responsible for food safety standards. Verification of applicable GB Food Safety Standards is required for all food safety parameters, including but not limited to additive limits, microbiology limits, contaminant limits, salt/sodium limits, allergen declaration/list, and labelling warnings.
rules.evidenceQuote: 食品安全标准

rules.limit: Verification required via GSFA Online Database
rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the database and its Preamble for specific provisions.
rules.evidenceQuote: The 'Codex General Standard for Food Additives' (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods

rules.limit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and CCCF established levels
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: Codex establishes Maximum Levels (MLs) for contaminants in food or feed commodities to protect human health, keeping levels as low as possible based on sound scientific evidence. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses these levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.limit: Follow Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: A specific Code of Practice (CXC 78-2017) is available for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices.
rules.evidenceQuote: CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices CCCF 2017

rules.sourceTitle: Database - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: The use of food additives and their conditions of use in the EU are governed by the Union list of food additives, detailed in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008. Specific limits and conditions must be verified through the Additives Database.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii), their toxins and metabolites (e.g., staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 establishes food safety criteria and process hygiene criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins, and metabolites in specific foods. Specific limits and conditions must be verified within the regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.parameter: Allergen declaration on labels of prepackaged foodstuffs

rules.notes: This technical regulation specifies microbiological limits for foodstuffs and food ingredients.
rules.evidenceQuote: This GSO technical regulation is concerned with microbiological limits for some foodstuffs intended for human consumption and for some food ingredients used in food industry.

rules.limit: Must be assessed for safety by SFA
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

rules.limit: Different limits apply depending on the additive and food product type; verification required
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Use the Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives to learn more.
rules.evidenceQuote: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products.

rules.limit: All food businesses must comply with regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

rules.parameter: Multiple Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
rules.limit: Use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to determine permitted levels
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food (e.g. sorbic acid or benzoic acid), use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels that should be used.

rules.limit: Do not import or sell food products containing non-permitted additives
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: An application can be submitted to SFA to evaluate the use of a non-permitted additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.limit: Verification required using SFA's List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Always check your additives against the list before importing or selling the food product.
rules.evidenceQuote: Download the List of Permitted Food Additives or use the Food Additives Search tool to check if your additives are allowed.

rules.limit: Food businesses must be aware of common contaminants and conduct laboratory tests as needed
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Suppliers may provide test information, or samples can be sent to SFA-recognized labs.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food businesses must be aware of the contaminants that are commonly found in their food products and conduct laboratory tests as needed.

rules.limit: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.limit: All food businesses must comply with regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents i.e. contaminants in food.

rules.limit: Do not import or sell food products containing contaminants exceeding regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.foodCategory: Meat and any food derived from meat
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Prohibition is under Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of the following compounds: diethylstibestrol (...) hexoestrol (...) dienoestrol

rules.parameter: Microbiological standards, heavy metals, mycotoxins, marine biotoxins, veterinary drug residues, pesticide residues, other incidental constituents
rules.limit: Verification of specific limits is required by referring to relevant SFA documents
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Specific documents are available for each contaminant type.
rules.evidenceQuote: For more details, please refer to the relevant documents below: Microbiological standards, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, Other incidental constituents.

rules.limit: Verification of Maximum Residue Limits (MRLs) is required
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: MRLs are available for download from the SFA website and will be included in future amendments of the Food Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.limit: Must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Applies only to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.limit: Must comply with general labelling requirements
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: Your food or food product must also comply with the labelling requirements.

rules.limit: Verification of microbiological standards is required
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Separate standards apply for ready-to-eat and non-ready-to-eat food.
rules.evidenceQuote: Microbiological standards Ready-to-eat food Non-ready-to-eat food (till 3 Mar 2024) Non-ready-to-eat food (from 4 Mar 2024)

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: This is a clarification on testing requirements, not a limit.
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: Specific use restrictions for γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) as a food ingredient must be verified against the full regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient

rules.parameter: Cephalopods and cephalopod products, Spiral shells and Spiral shell products thereof, Seeds and seed products (excluding highly refined/purified sunflower seed oil), Kiwis and kiwi products thereof
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Food allergen labeling is recommended for prepackaged foods containing these allergens as an ingredient or food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: Besides the mandatory labeling allergens, food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish, and sulphite
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Food allergen labeling is required for prepackaged foods containing these allergens as an ingredient or food additive. For sulphite, labeling is required if the residue sulphur dioxide is 10 mg/kg or more.
rules.evidenceQuote: For prepackaged foods, food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive... Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish, and sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food.

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods
rules.notes: Specific limits for pesticide residues in foods must be verified against the full standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products
rules.notes: Specific limits for pesticide residues in animal products must be verified against the full standard. Relevant for processed foods/snacks containing animal products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: If there is a risk of a food product being affected by allergen cross-contamination, a precautionary statement is suggested, such as 'The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○' or synonymous terms.
rules.evidenceQuote: If there is a risk of a food product being affected by allergen cross-contamination... the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.

rules.sourceTitle: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products
rules.notes: Specific requirements for nutrition claims on prepackaged food products must be verified against the full regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products

rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: Specific labeling requirements for γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) as a food ingredient must be verified against the full regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations on Good Hygiene Practice for Food
rules.notes: General requirements for good hygiene practices in food production to ensure safety and control microbiology. Specific microbiological limits would be detailed within the full regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Regulations on Good Hygiene Practice for Food

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Specific limits and permitted uses for food additives must be verified against relevant regulations (e.g., Notifications of the Ministry of Public Health, FDA Notifications).
rules.evidenceQuote: วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Specific standards for contaminants in food must be verified against relevant regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารปนเปื้อน

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Specific standards for toxins and veterinary drug residues in food must be verified against relevant regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารพิษ-ยาสัตว์ตกค้าง

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: All food products must comply with regulations concerning food labelling and nutrition labelling, which implicitly covers allergen declarations.
rules.evidenceQuote: การแสดงฉลากอาหารและฉลากโภชนาการ

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Certain foods are explicitly prohibited from production, import, or sale. Verification is required to identify these specific foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: อาหารที่ห้ามผลิต นำเข้า หรือจำหน่าย

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Specific standards for pathogenic microorganisms in food must be verified against relevant regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารด้านจุลินทรีย์ที่ทำให้เกิดโรค

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additive Listings | FDA
rules.notes: Specific approved food and color additives, their uses, and limits are detailed in FDA regulations and inventories.
rules.evidenceQuote: Approved Food and Color Additives and Petitions Under Review, Food and Color Additives Final Rules, Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) Citations for Color Additives, Food Ingredients and Packaging

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: The Substances Added to Food inventory includes these, but the full regulations must be consulted for specific limits and conditions of use.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively)

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: GRAS substances are listed in FDA regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184)

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Prohibited substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189).
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food

rules.foodCategory: seasonings, snack foods, processed foods (packaged foods, dietary supplements)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame. Sesame was added effective January 1, 2023.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool | FDA
rules.notes: This is a guidance level.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm

rules.foodCategory: Foods, Brazil nuts, Peanuts and peanut products, Pistachio nuts
rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool | FDA
rules.notes: This is an action level.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb, Aflatoxins, total Brazil nuts Action Level 20 ppb, Aflatoxins, total Peanuts and peanut products Action Level 20 ppb, Aflatoxins, total Pistachio nuts Action Level 20 ppb

rules.parameter: Various chemical contaminants (e.g., toxic elements, mycotoxins, persistent pesticides, radionuclides)
rules.limit: Verification required against the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool and referenced guidance documents/regulations
rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool | FDA
rules.notes: The tool provides a consolidated list of contaminant levels including tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list of these levels. Contaminant levels listed in the transparency tool include tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) parts 109 and 509.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Foods labeled 'gluten-free' must meet a defined standard for gluten content. Specific compliance requirements exist for fermented and hydrolyzed foods (effective 2020-08-12).
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling... that items labeled “gluten-free” meet a defined standard for gluten content. On August 12, 2020, the FDA issued a final rule to establish compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods... bearing the “gluten-free” claim.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Advisory statements are not required by law and should only be used to address unavoidable cross-contact after good manufacturing practices have been incorporated. They must be truthful and not misleading.
rules.evidenceQuote: Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility... and must be truthful and not misleading.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Facilities must implement allergen preventive controls as per the CGMP & PC rule (21 CFR part 117) to prevent allergen cross-contact and undeclared allergens, including written procedures.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA’s “Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food” rule (CGMP & PC rule, 21 CFR part 117) establishes requirements... for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Cochineal extract and carmine must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine... must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.foodCategory: seasonings, snack foods, processed foods (packaged foods, dietary supplements)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The food source of all major food allergens must be declared. This includes the specific type of tree nut, fish, and Crustacean shellfish. Declaration can be in parentheses after the ingredient or in a 'contains' statement immediately after or next to the ingredient list.
rules.evidenceQuote: FALCPA requires that foods or ingredients that contain a "major food allergen" be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source. The law also requires that the type of tree nut... and the species of fish... and Crustacean shellfish... to be declared. The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient... OR Immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: All products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 must identify it on their labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Sulfiting agents must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration is ≥10 ppm total sulfites.
rules.evidenceQuote: Various sulfiting agents... must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.

rules.sourceTitle: Vietnam Government Document Portal
rules.notes: Specific additive limits for seasoning, snack food, and processed foods need to be searched and verified within this official government document portal, under 'Y tế - Sức khỏe' (Health - Healthcare) issued by 'Bộ Y tế' (Ministry of Health).
rules.evidenceQuote: Hệ thống văn bản... Lĩnh vực: Y tế - Sức khỏe... Cơ quan ban hành: Bộ Y tế
